story.logline: A tired night cleaner follows a rule-breaking elevator to an erased office floor and finds a maintenance request written in her own handwriting.
story.dramaticQuestion: Will Mara treat the impossible as another broken system, or finally refuse the job that has been consuming her life?

beats.title: The exception
beats.purpose: Make the impossible behave like a precise workplace defect.

beats.title: The evidence
beats.purpose: Connect the missing floor to Mara through a physical record.

beats.title: The refusal
beats.purpose: Turn the supernatural premise into an earned human choice.

characters.name: Mara
wardrobeLock: yellow rubber gloves tucked into belt

scenes.title: Service elevator
scenes.purpose: Present the impossible floor as a repeatable mechanical exception.

scenes.title: Erased office
scenes.purpose: Reveal the personal maintenance request and Mara's refusal.

shots.title: The unlisted chime
shots.intent: Hook on one precise mechanical event that should not exist.
shots.subject: Mara holding a mop beside a stainless service elevator
shots.action: the floor display changes from B2 to a blank square and the doors open
shots.environment: a fluorescent-lit concrete service corridor with damp wheel marks

camera.movement: locked tripod with a two-centimeter vibration when the elevator stops
camera.framing: Mara left third, elevator doors filling the right half
camera.focusBehavior: deep focus keeps Mara and the floor display readable

physics: elevator decelerates with believable cabin mass
mop water remains pooled by gravity
doors travel on a stable mechanical track

beats.action: Mara drags the mop bucket into frame
the elevator chimes and the display becomes a blank square
doors open onto darkness while Mara checks her watch instead of recoiling

continuityLocks: same silver hair streak and navy jacket
same mop bucket position and wet wheel trail
display remains a blank square after the chime
imperfectionAnchors: faint wipe streaks on elevator steel
one fluorescent tube warms half a second late
mop wheel squeaks once under load
shots.dialogue: Mara, under her breath: Of course there is another floor.

shots.title: The practical test
shots.intent: Make Mara investigate the impossible with tired professional logic.
shots.subject: Mara at the threshold with one yellow glove now on her right hand
shots.action: she wedges the mop head between the doors, tests the floor, and steps across
shots.environment: the service elevator threshold opening onto an unlit carpeted office

camera.movement: slow eight-inch push following the mop head across the threshold
camera.framing: mop head foreground, Mara's face held in upper left
camera.focusBehavior: rack from the mop fibers to Mara's eyes after the floor holds

lighting.primarySource: service corridor fluorescents spilling through the doors
lighting.motivation: the only powered area is behind Mara

physics: mop handle flexes under hand pressure
elevator doors stop against the mop head safety sensor
carpet compresses under the wet mop fibers

beats.action: Mara slides the mop head across the threshold
she presses down twice and watches the carpet hold
she puts on one glove and steps through

continuityLocks: same silver hair streak and navy jacket
same mop and bucket remain visible behind her
imperfectionAnchors: water beads catch only the corridor spill
mop handle paint is chipped near the grip
carpet dust lifts in one narrow plume

soundDesignDirectives: mop fibers drag across steel and carpet
doors strain softly against safety stop
ventilation hum drops at the threshold

shots.title: The unsigned work order
shots.intent: Pay off the impossible floor with a specific choice rather than an explanation.
shots.subject: Mara beside a lone desk lamp and a handwritten maintenance log
shots.action: she recognizes her handwriting, crosses out the request, and leaves her keys on the page
shots.environment: a dust-coated office with cubicles disappearing into darkness

camera.movement: measured tilt from Mara's reflection in the lamp base to the logbook
camera.framing: handwriting and keys dominate the final lower-center composition
camera.focusBehavior: focus settles from reflected eye to ink, then holds on keys

lighting.motivation: the one powered object on the erased floor

physics: keys land with believable weight and stop against the paper ridge
ink crosses the paper fibers without glowing

beats.action: lamp reflection reveals Mara reading a request in her own handwriting
she draws one deliberate line through FIX ELEVATOR
she places her keys on the log and turns away as the lamp remains on

continuityLocks: same silver hair streak, navy jacket, and single right glove
same wet mop remains at the doorway in background
desk lamp position and handwritten page do not change
imperfectionAnchors: finger oil darkens the ledger edge
lamp base reflection is slightly warped
one key has a worn red plastic tag

soundDesignDirectives: keys land with one brass clack
